name: Montenegro

visa_free.notes: Citizens of EU member states may enter, transit, and stay up to 90 days with a valid passport; up to 30 days with a valid national identity card. Includes Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Croatia, Cyprus, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden.

visa_free.nationality: British Overseas Territories (Anguilla, Bermuda, British Virgin Islands, Cayman Islands, Falkland Islands, Gibraltar, Montserrat, Pitcairn, Saint Helena, Turks and Caicos, Hong Kong, Macao, etc.)
visa_free.notes: Holders of valid travel documents issued by British Overseas Territories authorities may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. List includes: Anguilla, Bermuda, British Antarctic Territory, British Indian Ocean Territory, British Virgin Islands, Cayman Islands, Falkland Islands, Gibraltar, Montserrat, Pitcairn Island, Saint Helena, Ascension Island, Tristan da Cunha, South Georgia and Sandwich Islands, Turks and Caicos Islands, Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR.

visa_policy.notes: Montenegro is progressively aligning its visa policy with EU Regulation 2018/1806 as part of its EU accession process. Countries removed from visa-free access (as of late 2025): Kuwait, Armenia, Uzbekistan, Egypt. Turkey was suspended Oct 2025 then reinstated Dec 2025 with reduced 30-day limit. Azerbaijan: visa-free access was terminated effective January 15, 2026 per 23 December 2025 cabinet decree — as of June 2026 official embassy page confirms visa is required. Russia and Belarus retain 30-day visa-free access under bilateral agreements (confirmed June 2026). Netherlands Antilles entry removed: that entity was dissolved on 10 October 2010 and no longer exists as a travel document issuing authority. Bulgaria and Romania conditional_access entries removed: both became full Schengen members per Council Decision 2024/210 and were formally delisted from separate Montenegro visa listings at the 98th Cabinet session — their holders are now covered by the Schengen credential entries. Duplicate consolidated credential entries (one set from visas article, one from embassies-and-consulates overview) have been de-duplicated. Montenegro does NOT currently have an operational e-visa system; VFS Global visa application centres opened in India and Kyrgyzstan in October 2025 for in-person applications.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid Schengen visa may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days. As of 2024/2025, Bulgaria and Romania are full Schengen members; their visas are now covered by this entry.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of the United States of America may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of the Republic of Ireland may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of the Commonwealth of Australia may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of Japan may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of Canada may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid visa of New Zealand may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiry of the visa if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen area residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by a Schengen zone country may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days. As of 2024/2025, Bulgaria and Romania are full Schengen members; their residence permits are now covered by this entry.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by the United States of America may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by the Republic of Ireland may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by the Commonwealth of Australia may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by Japan may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by Canada may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

credential.subtype: any valid New Zealand residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid residence permit issued by New Zealand may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the residence permit if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a valid APEC Business Travel Card issued by an APEC member state may enter, transit through, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days, and no longer than until the expiration of the APEC Business Travel Card if the validity period is shorter than 30 days.

credential.subtype: EU travel document issued under 1951 Refugee Convention or 1954 Stateless Persons Convention

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of travel documents issued by EU member states on the basis of the 1951 Convention relating to the Status of Refugees or the 1954 Convention relating to the Legal Status of Stateless Persons may enter, transit, and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days without a visa.

credential.subtype: business passport or organized tourist group membership

conditional_access.conditions: 30 days visa-free for holders of business passports or members of organized tourist groups.
Holders of valid United Nations Laissez-Passer documents may stay 90 days visa-free.

cbi.notes: Montenegro's Citizenship by Investment Programme was active from 2019 to 31 December 2022. The programme has been officially concluded and no new applications can be submitted. The standard path to Montenegrin citizenship requires 10 years of continuous lawful residence (5 years temporary + 5 years permanent). The MIA (Montenegro Investment Agency) page at mia.gov.me/en/invest-in-mne/economic-citizenship/ returned 404 at time of research, but the programme closure is confirmed by multiple official government references.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Based on Property Ownership
programs.notes: Third-country nationals may obtain temporary residence in Montenegro based on ownership of real estate with a taxable value of at least EUR 150,000 (as determined by the Tax Authority's transfer tax assessment). This threshold was introduced by legislation adopted December 2025 in force from January 17, 2026. Owners must prove both ownership and actual use of the property, and all property tax obligations must be settled. Permits are valid for up to 1 year and are renewable. Grandfathering clause: foreigners who obtained property-based residence before the law took effect may renew without meeting the new valuation requirement. Path to permanent residence: 5 consecutive years of lawful temporary residence (note: time spent on property-disposal residence does NOT count toward the 5-year requirement for permanent residence). Path to citizenship: 10 years total lawful continuous residence as a general naturalization rule.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Temporary Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreigners employed or working electronically for a foreign company, or own a company not registered in Montenegro. Must provide: proof of financial means (income of at least 3x Montenegro minimum wage, approximately EUR 1,800–2,400/month), accommodation, health insurance covering Montenegro, valid travel document (3+ months beyond permit period), no criminal conviction exceeding 6 months imprisonment.
programs.processing_time: Within 40 days from submission of complete application
programs.notes: Permit duration: initial up to 2 years; first extension: up to 2 additional years; new permit may be requested 6 months after expiration. Family reunification: close family members (spouses, minor children under 18) may join permit holders. Application submitted in person at the Ministry of Interior office in applicant's municipality of residence. Programme announced under 'Programme for Attracting Digital Nomads in Montenegro until 2025 with Action Plan for 2022' extended through 2026. Long-stay Type D visa category covers digital nomads. Official portal: digitalnomads.gov.me.

agreements: Western Balkans Freedom of Movement with Identity Cards Agreement (Berlin Process, 2022) — Montenegro, Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo, North Macedonia, Serbia
CEFTA (Central European Free Trade Agreement) — goods/trade, members include Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia
EU Accession Candidate — Montenegro has been an EU candidate since December 2010, with accession negotiations ongoing since June 2012

visa_free.name: Albania
visa_free.notes: Also valid with ID card.

visa_free.name: China
visa_free.notes: Visa-free for ordinary passport holders for business, tourism, family visits, exchanges, and transit. Valid from 30 Nov 2024 to 31 Dec 2026.

visa_free.name: Serbia
visa_free.notes: Also valid with ID card.

visa_free.name: Turkey
visa_free.notes: Passport must be valid at least 60 days beyond the 90-day stay. 90 days within 180-day period.

eta.name: Schengen Area countries
eta.notes: ETIAS authorization required from travellers entering Schengen countries on a visa-free basis. Montenegro citizens remain visa-free but must obtain ETIAS before travel (applicable from 2025 per EU rollout schedule).

outbound_official.notes: Montenegro's Ministry of Foreign Affairs publishes outbound visa regime information through a per-country page system at https://www.gov.me/en/diplomatic-missions/diplomatic-missions-to-montenegro/[country]. The hub page at https://www.gov.me/en/article/overview-of-visa-regimes links to both 'Overview of visa regimes for citizens of Montenegro' (the inbound country pages) and a Decree on Visa Regime document. Individual country pages contain exact visa regime language for Montenegro passport holders. This outbound list is not exhaustive — only countries with confirmed official gov.me pages are recorded here. Countries NOT confirmed visa-free at official source: United Kingdom (visa required), United States of America (visa required). China visa-free is time-limited (30 Nov 2024 – 31 Dec 2026). Montenegro citizens with biometric passports have been visa-free for the Schengen area since December 2009 (90 days in any 180-day period). mvp.gov.me (Ministry of Foreign Affairs domain) was connection-refused during research; information sourced from gov.me portal instead.

validation_notes: REMOVED: 'Netherlands Antilles' visa_free entry — this political entity was dissolved on 10 October 2010 and no longer issues travel documents; the entry was invalid.

REMOVED: Bulgaria and Romania conditional_access entries (visa and residence_permit) — both became full Schengen members per Council Decision 2024/210; the 98th Cabinet session press release confirmed their removal from separate Montenegro visa listings. Their holders are now covered by the Schengen credential entries (source: https://www.gov.me/en/article/press-release-from-the-98th-cabinet-session).

REMOVED: Two duplicate consolidated conditional_access entries ('US|UK|Ireland' combo entry and Schengen combo entry from embassies-and-consulates overview) — these were redundant with the individually itemised credential entries from the visas article.

UPDATED: Azerbaijan gap clarified — official embassy page (https://www.gov.me/en/diplomatic-missions/embassies-and-consulates-of-montenegro/azerbaijan) now confirms visa is required; Azerbaijan was removed from visa-free access effective January 15, 2026 per 23 December 2025 cabinet decree.

CONFIRMED: Russia 30-day visa-free — live official embassy page (https://www.gov.me/en/diplomatic-missions/embassies-and-consulates-of-montenegro/russian-federation) confirms 30-day visa-free under bilateral agreement.

CONFIRMED: Belarus 30-day visa-free — live official embassy page (https://www.gov.me/en/diplomatic-missions/embassies-and-consulates-of-montenegro/belarus) confirms 30-day visa-free under bilateral agreement.
CONFIRMED: Nauru entry retained — confirmed by 98th Cabinet session press release as added to align with EU Regulation (EU) 2018/1806.
CONFIRMED: Turkey 30-day visa-free — reinstated December 2025 with reduced limit; no further changes found.
UPDATED: Source URL for Russia and Belarus entries updated to their dedicated official embassy pages for better citability.
CONFIRMED: No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system operational as of June 2026.
CONFIRMED: South Africa requires a visa for Montenegro — not in visa_free list, which is correct.
gaps: MIA economic citizenship page (mia.gov.me/en/invest-in-mne/economic-citizenship/) returned HTTP 404 — CBI programme closure confirmed from other official gov.me references but programme details not directly verifiable from live page.
mvp.gov.me (Ministry of Foreign Affairs) was connection-refused during this research session — data sourced from gov.me portal instead.

The actual Decree on Visa Regime DOCX files (wapi.gov.me and gov.me documents) are not directly readable via WebFetch; country-by-country list obtained from gov.me article pages and individual country embassy pages.
Complete visa-free country list may not be exhaustive — the official decree DOCX was inaccessible; gov.me article pages provided substantial detail but enumeration relies on these summaries.
Armenia, Uzbekistan, Egypt: removed from visa-free list confirmed by 98th Cabinet session press release; exact effective dates not extracted.
Kuwait: removed from visa-free list as confirmed by official press release; exact effective date not extracted.
Digital nomad minimum income threshold (3x minimum wage) confirmed by multiple secondary sources but exact figure not found verbatim on digitalnomads.gov.me page.
No e-visa or ETA system currently operational — Montenegro is developing an e-visa system with EU funding (IPA III project) but it was not live at time of this research.

Kyrgyzstan diplomatic and service passport visa waiver agreement: signed September 26, 2024 in New York and entered into force May 8, 2025 per news agency reports, but the official gov.me country page for Kyrgyzstan does not reflect this agreement. Cannot record as a conditional_access entry under official-source-first rules.

Non-ordinary passport exemptions (Armenia diplomatic, China diplomatic, Guinea, Indonesia, Iran, Mongolia, Morocco, North Korea, Pakistan, Tajikistan, Thailand, Vietnam, Zimbabwe): sourced only from Wikipedia/aggregators, not verified on official gov.me pages. Not recorded per official-source-first rules.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption: no evidence found on any official gov.me page. Not recorded.
Interpol Travel Document (ITD) holders: Wikipedia lists as 90-day visa-free but not confirmed on official gov.me pages during this research session. Not recorded.
